meaning: n.惊人的事物,天才(特指神童),奇观,奇事
examples: She was a child prodigy, giving concerts before she was a teenager.
The Russian tennis prodigy is well on the way to becoming the youngest world champion of all time.
The singer Michael Jackson's story, although unusually brutal and extreme, is illumination when considering musical prodigy.
The stakes couldn't be higher for Nadal, a tennis prodigy like few prodigies before him.
Best festival dance act went to The Prodigy and Dizzee Rascal was awarded best urban act.
